perturbation.original_text: Franchisee agrees to carry at its expense during the Term of this Agreement Comprehensive General Liability insurance, including Products Liability and Broad Form Contractual Liability, in an amount which is at all times the local equivalent of not less than One Million U.S. Dollars (U.S. $1,000,000.00) per occurrence for bodily injury and Five Hundred Thousand U.S. Dollars (U.S. $500,000.00) per occurrence for property damage, or in such increased amounts as BKC may reasonably request from time to time during the Term of this Agreement. Each policy will name BKC, and its subsidiaries, affiliated and parent companies as an additional insured, and will provide hat the policy cannot be canceled without thirty (30) days prior written notice to BKC, will insure against the liability of BKC for both its and Franchisee's acts or omissions, and will insure the contractual liability of Franchisee under paragraph 13.3 Additionally, Franchisee agrees to carry, at Franchisee's expense, umbrella coverage in an amount which is at all times the equivalent of One Million U.S. Dollars (U.S. $1,000,000) over the basic
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee agrees to carry at its expense during the Term of this Agreement General Liability insurance in an amount to be determined by the Franchisee. Each policy will name BKC, and its subsidiaries, affiliated and parent companies as an additional insured, and will provide hat the policy cannot be canceled without thirty (30) days prior written notice to BKC, will insure against the liability of BKC for both its and Franchisee's acts or omissions, and will insure the contractual liability of Franchisee under paragraph 13.3 Additionally, Franchisee agrees to carry, at Franchisee's expense, umbrella coverage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'Comprehensive' aspect of the insurance requirement, the specific liability coverages, and the minimum coverage amounts, the contract is weakened and shifts the determination of the insurance amount to the Franchisee's discretion, potentially leading to insufficient coverage. This omission introduces legal risk as it may not meet minimum insurance requirements mandated by law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York State law requires specific minimum insurance coverage for businesses.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY CLS Ins § 3420 mandates specific minimum coverage amounts and terms for liability insurance policies issued in New York. By removing the explicit mention of minimum coverage amounts and the type of liability insurance required, the modified text contradicts this law as it allows for policies that may not meet the statutory requirements for coverage, potentially leaving BKC and the Franchisee underinsured and in violation of state law.

perturbation.justification: The modified text omits key aspects of the insurance requirement, including the 'Comprehensive' aspect, specific liability coverages, and minimum coverage amounts. This directly contradicts the intention of ensuring adequate protection, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation. Although the provided law snippets are not helpful, the omission of specific coverage details and amounts, which would normally be legally mandated, creates a significant legal loophole and risk of non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, this Agreement shall automatically terminate without any further notice or opportunity to cure under Section 17.1.1 above and BKC shall, subject to the provisions of Subsection 17.6 below, have the right to claim lost royalties and advertising contributions, and shall also have all other rights and remedies available under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, this Agreement shall automatically terminate and BKC shall have the right to claim lost royalties and advertising contributions, and shall also have all other rights and remedies available under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "without any further notice or opportunity to cure under Section 17.1.1 above", the contract eliminates the franchisee's right to receive a notice and opportunity to cure the default before the termination takes effect. This can result in premature termination and loss of business.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York law requires notification and opportunity to cure for contract defaults.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY Real Prop Acts Law § 753 provides tenants with the right to cure defaults in lease agreements. While this is specifically for real property, the principle of providing notice and an opportunity to cure is a broadly applied concept in contract law. By removing the "opportunity to cure," the modified text contradicts the spirit and underlying legal principle of ensuring fairness and preventing unjust terminations, thereby increasing the risk of legal challenges based on due process and fairness.

perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the "opportunity to cure," which, as the Law Explanation details, contradicts the spirit and underlying legal principle of ensuring fairness and preventing unjust terminations. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing and Scraped Law Snippet 2 is a login page for LexisNexis and doesn't provide specific legal text, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation highlight the removal of a standard protection, which is further supported by the definition of 'Omission Legal Contradiction' and how that affects consumer protection. The original text ensures a notice and an opportunity to cure, and the changed text removes this right, thus increasing the risk of legal challenges.
